- 4.2 Support structures
- 2m-high gantry truss with full-width checker plate walkway extending from the access ladder across the width of the VMS or to a point 1m past the far side of the furthest lane signal unit.
- The gantry truss shall be level across the carriageway and the minimum clearance to the lowest point of the gantry structure shall be at least 6m above the highest point of the carriageway below.
- Load calculations for the gantry structures shall include an allowance for equipment weight plus 25% to allow for future alterations.
- The design shall in particular comply with the requirements of
- AS/NZS 1170,
- NZS 3101.1&2:2006 Concrete structures standard
- NZS 3404 Parts 1 and 2:1997 Steel structures standard.
- Walkways shall be provided with a minimum of three safety rails on each side at 450mm, 900mm and 1350m.

_Captured Thursday, 12 November 2020 12:44 PM · General › Geotechnical & Foundations_

**Diagrams**
![spt-test](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/gen_565_spt-test_1.png)

- Reference documents;
- Field-guide-sheet-description-of-soil-and-rock-2005-3
- Soils chart.250203
- How is the SPT performed?
- A split-barrel sampler is driven into the soil at the bottom of a borehole using a hammer of specific weight and fall height.
- The number of blows required to drive the sampler 300 mm into the soil is recorded as the SPT-N value.
- Parameters measured?
- Provides the SPT-N value, which is correlated with soil properties like density, strength, and compressibility.
- Accuracy and Reliability
- Less reliable for cohesionless soils (e.g., loose sands) and very soft clays.
- Application in Foundation Design
- Widely used in traditional foundation design for estimating bearing capacity and settlement,
- Test procedure
- Decide on
- the total depth
- Groundwater or hard strata (10m)
- intermediate test depth
- The Test shall be made at every change in the stratum or at intervals of not more than 1.5 meters whichever is less.

_Captured Sunday, 7 May 2023 4:22 PM · General › Geotechnical & Foundations_

![cpt-test](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/gen_566_cpt-test_4.png)

- Traditional soil classification systems (e.g., USCS) are based on laboratory-determined physical characteristics, such as grain size distribution and plasticity, which are measured on remoulded samples.
- The CPT measurements provide a repeatable index of the aggregate behaviour of the in-situ soil in the immediate area of the probe.
- The prediction of soil type based on CPT measurements is referred to as the Soil Behaviour Type (SBT).
- Reference documents;
- CPT-Guide-7th-Final-SMALL
- NZGS
- Estimation of in-situ water content, void ratio, dry unit weight and porosity using CPT for saturated sands
- Design files;
- Pile capacity check ms
- How is the CPT performed?
- A cone penetrometer is pushed into the soil at a constant rate using a hydraulic system.
- It continuously measures the
- Cone res

- Reference Documents: Models and pricing for GitHub Copilot - GitHub Docs
- WSP GitHub Enterprise
- WSP GitHub Enterprise Managed User (EMU)
- Water Design and Draughting Efficiency Initiative
- Hi team, General
- This is the introduction and first update regarding a process underway to transition water design & draughting to a nationally consistent delivery model across the Water Sector, Regions (Water), and the Water Global Capability Centres (GCC).
- Our work will focus on how we consistently work
- efficiently
- together to produce
- high quality and consistent outputs
- .
- It is not a change in team structures etc. - this is about the supporting systems and processes that will allow anyone, anywhere to
- work seamlessly across clients and WSP
- .
- Our initial focus is pipeline design & draughting in Civil 3D (but this will expand over time).

**What is happening now and in the coming weeks**
- Digital leads from Water and Transport are in the process of adopting a single CAD standard from one of the many in use around the business - this should be finalised soon.
- Digital leads from Water and Transport are
